channel.title: InsurTech Intelligence
channel.description: Noticias globales de insurtech, actualizadas cada 6 horas.

item.title: Efectibank quiere trasladar su “fábrica de créditos” al negocio digital
item.description: Efectibank, el nuevo banco digital del Grupo EFE, quiere competir en el mercado peruano con un portafolio amplio que incluya créditos al consumo, un nutrido marketplace embebido y otros productos destinados a captar más usuarios. La institución, antes conocida como Financiera Efectiva, recibió recientemente el aval de la Superintendencia de Bancos, Seguros y AFP (SBS) del país andino para operar con un perfil bancario digital. Regístrate gratis o inicia sesión para leer el artículo completo y descubrir: Perspectivas estratégicas sobre innovación, regulación y tendencias en banca digital y fintech Entrevistas con líderes que están transformando el sector financiero en la región Reportes y estudios que anticipan los cambios clave del mercado Acceso prioritario a información para tomar decisiones informadas y fortalecer tu liderazgo Únete a la comunidad de ejecutivos que marcan la diferencia en la transformación digital de la banca.

item.title: Honda Recalling 99,000 US Vehicles Over Airbag Issues
item.description: Honda Motor is recalling 98,892 vehicles in the United States over a defect involving unintentional deployment of air bags, the U.S. Related: Stellantis Recalling 419K US Vehicles for Improper Side Air Bag Deployment (Reporting by Sharma in Bengaluru; Editing by Subhranshu Sahu) Get the latest insurance news sent straight to your inbox. National Highway Traffic Safety Administration (NHTSA) said on Friday.

item.title: Verisk: Hail Volatility, Aging Roofs Driving Higher Severity
item.description: Roof Report shows everyday wind and hail events and aging roofs are driving higher residential replacement severity. Roofs that are in moderate to poor condition show roughly 60% higher loss costs than roofs in good or excellent condition, according to Verisk. Related: State Farm Paid a ‘Hail’ of a Lot of Claims in 2025 Average residential roof replacement costs hit $17,631 in 2025, with repair costs averaging $4,699, the Verisk report out last week shows.

item.title: Property Fight That Hit Several Banks Spurs $1.34 Billion Award
item.description: A years-long dispute over a portfolio of commercial properties in Southern California that sparked credit concerns at several banks last year has led to a roughly $1.34 billion arbitration award. In separate lawsuits, banks including Western Alliance Bancorp and Zions Bancorp NA claimed that a tangled web of investors used some of the same properties to get multiple loans without full disclosure, leading the lenders to believe they had first-lien rights. The discovery of the problem led the banks to disclose potential loan losses late last year.

item.title: Your Tech Stack Is Your Recruiting Strategy (Whether You Know It or Not)
item.description: The top 10 insurers in the world employ 56% of all AI talent in the insurance industry. That is not a distribution curve; it is a verdict on the state of the industry. While there are thousands of carriers globally, specialized expertise is aggressively clustered within this tiny elite.

item.title: Generali GC&C hires Zurich’s Kai Ao as head of business development
item.description: To view the full article and get unlimited access to InsuranceAsia News's exclusive insight across 5,000+ articles

item.title: Philippine non-life profit gains continue as premium growth moderates amid catastrophe pressure: AM Best
item.description: To view the full article and get unlimited access to InsuranceAsia News's exclusive insight across 5,000+ articles

item.title: Florida State Board likely to reduce property cat ILS investments, keep diversifying
item.description: The Florida State Board of Administration, which manages allocations to insurance-linked securities funds and reinsurance strategies on behalf of the giant Florida Retirement System Pension Plan, has said it is likely to reduce its exposure to property catastrophe risk as expected mean loss returns have declined. As we’ve been reporting over the last year, the Florida State Board has been actively diversifying within the insurance-linked securities and reinsurance asset class, as it looks to create a more balanced portfolio with less weighting towards property catastrophe risks. More recently, the Florida State Board disclosed a new $150 million allocation to the latest vintage of a life settlement strategy it had allocated to before , the Miravast ILS Credit Opportunities III LP fund.

item.title: Beazley expands Bermuda presence with Wunderlich appointment as Head of ART
item.description: Beazley, a large specialist insurer, has appointed Stefan Wunderlich as Head of Alternative Risk Transfer (ART), where he will take on this new responsibility alongside his existing role as Head of Parametric and will relocate to Bermuda, subject to immigration approval. Earlier this year, Beazley also announced that its Head of Third-Party Capital Richard Gray would be relocating to Bermuda to take on the responsibilities of launching and running operations there, as well as the new cyber ILS venture for the company. Wunderlich joined Beazley in June 2025 following a 20-year career at global reinsurer Swiss Re, where he specialised in modeling and underwriting property and natural catastrophe risks.

item.title: Swiss Re names Martin Zingg to lead Alternative Capital Partners (ACP) as Minter to retire
item.description: Swiss Re has appointed long-standing senior employee Martin Zingg as the new head of its Alternative Capital Partners (ACP) division, as incumbent to the role Chris Minter is set to retire from the global reinsurance company. The position of Head of the Alternative Capital Partners unit was taken on by Minter in 2024 , having previously been the Head of Principal Investments & Acquisitions at Swiss Re. Alternative Capital Partners (ACP) was established in 2019 as a division that oversees all of Swiss Re’s activities with alternative capital investors, as well as in the catastrophe bond and insurance-linked securities (ILS) market, including its structuring and ILS fund management operations.

item.title: Africa Re’s GWP rises 9.9% in Q1’26
item.description: Africa Re said, “The slower translation of written premium growth into IFRS 17 revenue was mainly due to higher reserves for unexpired risks.” For Q1’26, under IFRS 17, reinsurance revenue declined by 4.8% to $249.5 million from $262.1 million in Q1’25. Africa Re explained that investment and other income under IFRS 4 amounted to $16.6 million, down 23.9% year-on-year, largely attributable to capital losses on listed equities and bonds, whose valuations were adversely affected by geopolitical tensions in the Middle East. Nigeria domiciled African Reinsurance Corporation has reported a 9.9% increase in gross written premiums (GWP) for the first quarter of 2026 to $316.3 million, compared to $287.8 million in Q1’25.

item.title: Beazley appoints Wunderlich as Head of Alternative Risk Transfer, Bermuda
item.description: Beazley, a large specialist insurer, has expanded Stefan Wunderlich’s responsibilities and appointed him as Head of Alternative Risk Transfer (ART) alongside his existing role of Head of Parametric. For the new role, Wunderlich will be relocating to Bermuda, subject to immigration approval. The move strengthens the insurer’s commitment to Bermuda, building on the recent confirmation of Beazley’s new office at Ninety-One, 91 Front Street, opening in June 2027.

item.title: J-ICS reform key driver of steady build-up in Japan life insurers’ reinsurance usage
item.description: A new AM Best report has revealed that Japan’s life insurers have increasingly relied on reinsurance in recent years, driven largely by the segment preparing for the new J-ICS solvency regime, which took effect at the end of March 2026. Cynthia Ang, senior industry research analyst, AM Best, added, “Japanese life insurers have been increasingly using asset-intensive reinsurance to transfer investment, longevity and insurance risks from capital-intensive annuity and long-term life insurance blocks to third-party reinsurers ahead of the implementation of J-ICS. “The maturity and size of Japan’s life/annuity insurance market make it an attractive opportunity for reinsurers providing asset-intensive reinsurance solutions.” Under the J-ICS, the new economic value-based solvency ratio will reportedly be more sensitive to fluctuations in interest rates, lapses, asset-liability management mismatches and longevity/mortality risks.

item.title: SEC Moves to Scrap Biden-Era Rule on Climate Risk Disclosures
item.description: The US Securities and Exchange Commission wants to formally kill rules it’s now calling a “dramatic overreach” of its own authority that would have required public companies to report greenhouse gas emissions and climate-related risks to their operations. The 2024 rules, which never went into effect, were “unsound as a matter of policy,” the SEC said May 29 in a proposal that would officially dismantle the Biden-era regulations. The 2024 corporate disclosure requirements, issued under former Chairman Gary Gensler, called on companies to report their pollution as well as how climate-related risks, like the threat of rising sea levels, hurricanes, droughts or wildfires, affected their bottom lines.

item.title: Israel Expands Lebanon Assault With Iran-US Talks in Balance
item.description: Israel expanded its ground assault in Lebanon with its broadest incursion into the country in a quarter-century as Hezbollah — Iran’s most powerful regional ally — stepped up attacks on Israel’s north. According to the Israeli military, Hezbollah fired more than 300 “projectiles” at its soldiers in Lebanon and at northern Israel over the weekend. The latest escalation has shattered a brittle ceasefire declared after the Tehran-backed group attacked Israel in response to its war on Iran, which it launched with the US on Feb.

item.title: Europe Faces Biggest Food Price Shock in G7 From Hotter World
item.description: A price shock could see food prices rise 1 percentage point in the UK, but just 0.28 point in the US and 0.35 point in Japan. A “severe” shock to food prices from climate events could raise food prices in countries sharing the currency by 1.6 percentage points a year, the economic adviser said in a report. “Climate change and nature-loss are increasingly contributing to food price inflation and volatility, presenting a long-term risk to economies,” the authors said.

item.title: Standard Life completes £200M pension buy-in with Abbey Life scheme
item.description: Standard Life completed a £200 million bulk purchase annuity transaction with the Abbey Life Assurance Company Limited Staff Pension Scheme, securing the pensions of more than 1,500 members. Standard Life, which acquired Abbey Life in 2016, worked with the scheme’s trustee and advisers on the transaction. This marks a positive step in securing members’ benefits for the long term.” – Lauren Doherty, Pensions Risk Transfer Transaction Manager at Standard Life.

item.title: SAFE launches AI security posture management platform for enterprise AI tools
item.description: SAFE launched SAFE AI Security Posture Management (AI-SPM), a new platform designed to help enterprises monitor and manage cyber risks tied to AI tools such as ChatGPT, Claude, Gemini, and Copilot. With AI Security Posture Management, SAFE is helping organizations address one of the most important board-level issues in the AI era: understanding and managing AI-related cyber risk at scale.” – John Chambers, Founder and CEO of JC2 Ventures and former Chairman and CEO of Cisco. SAFE AI Security Posture Management gives enterprises the operational control needed to govern AI adoption responsibly.” – Michael Johnson, former CIO and CISO US Department of Energy, Capital One, and Meta Financial Technologies.

item.title: ADIA backs Sapiens
item.description: Sapiens announced that a subsidiary of the Abu Dhabi Investment Authority has taken a significant minority stake in the company as it accelerates its AI strategy for insurers and expands its global footprint. Sapiens, which serves more than 600 insurers globally, said its agentic AI platform is designed to automate workflows across underwriting, claims, pricing, risk management, and billing. “AI powered hyper-relevance is the new competitive advantage for insurers, and we are enabling this through agile intelligence and the ability to make decisions at the speed of thought.” – Mike Ettling, executive chairman and interim CEO of Sapiens.

item.title: Allianz Commercial continues Japan build out, taps Masahide Naito to lead property
item.description: To view the full article and get unlimited access to InsuranceAsia News's exclusive insight across 5,000+ articles

item.title: Peak Re ready for next phase of growth, embrace new era: Franz-Josef Hahn
item.description: To view the full article and get unlimited access to InsuranceAsia News's exclusive insight across 5,000+ articles

item.title: QBE appoints Andy Tsui to dual underwriting, people risk roles
item.description: To view the full article and get unlimited access to InsuranceAsia News's exclusive insight across 5,000+ articles

item.title: Bus Hits Cars in Virginia, Killing 5 Massachusetts People and Injuring 34
item.description: A bus crashed into vehicles slowing for a work zone on Interstate 95 in Virginia early Friday, killing five people and injuring dozens, including the driver, authorities said. All five of the people who died were in vehicles hit by the bus, and 44 people were taken to hospitals, including three in critical condition, police said. State police said the victims were a 45-year-old male, a 44-year-old female, a 13-year-old female and a 7-year-old male, all from Greenfield, Massachusetts.

item.title: Hadron names AXA XL and AIG veterans to leadership roles
item.description: Hadron Insurance announced the appointments of Liesel Carvajal as Group General Counsel and Ed McGough as Chief Information Officer as the specialty carrier continues to expand its delegated authority platform globally. Carvajal joins Hadron from AXA XL, where she served as General Counsel for the U.S. She previously held legal roles at Arch Insurance Group, AIG, and The Hartford.

item.title: Insurance Attorneys Flip $1M Hail Claim into Nearly $2M Suit for Contractor Interference
item.description: What began as a lawsuit filed against Church Mutual Insurance Company on a $1 million-plus claim dispute turned into a potential $2 million award against a contractor after a jury found the contractor intentionally interfered with the insurance contract and caused the litigation. District Court for the District of Colorado, the church sued the contractor for breach of contract and negligent misrepresentation, and the insurer sued for fraud and intentional interference of the insurance contract causing a breach of the misrepresentation clause and punitive damages. “I think this is the first time, certainly in Colorado, that I’ve seen a claim be brought for intentional interference with the insurance contract for breach of the misrepresentation clause and we were very intentional in how we brought that claim,” said Jeremy Moseley with Spencer Fane, who represented Church Mutual along with attorney Will Brophy.

item.title: Lavanya Mundayur returns to New India Assurance as chairman, managing director
item.description: To view the full article and get unlimited access to InsuranceAsia News's exclusive insight across 5,000+ articles

item.title: Tiptree, Warburg Pincus complete US$1.65bn Fortegra sale to DB Insurance
item.description: To view the full article and get unlimited access to InsuranceAsia News's exclusive insight across 5,000+ articles

item.title: HUB SmartCoverage becomes VIU by HUB in Canada
item.description: HUB International has rebranded its Canadian digital insurance platform HUB SmartCoverage to VIU by HUB Canada as part of an effort to unify its digital consumer insurance brand across North America. The platform, which has operated in Canada since 2001, allows consumers to compare quotes from multiple insurers, purchase coverage online, and access licensed brokers throughout the process. “This refresh reflects what we’ve built in Canada over many years and signals clearly where we’re going.

item.title: American Family files trademark for “EV Claims Advantage”
item.description: American Family Insurance has filed a trademark application for “EV Claims Advantage,” signaling a potential expansion of its electric vehicle insurance and claims capabilities. The trademark filing describes the offering as focused on “electric vehicle and electric vehicle charging station insurance,” including claims support and policy administration. Schulz on behalf of American Family Mutual Insurance Company, S.I.

item.title: MetLife Japan raises starting salaries for office-based employees
item.description: MetLife Japan will revise its compensation structure for office-based employees starting June 2026, increasing fixed monthly pay and reducing the emphasis on seasonal bonuses as part of an effort to provide more stable compensation and support long term employee retention. Under the revised structure, the starting salary for university graduates in office-based roles such as agency consulting and actuarial positions will increase from ¥278,000 to ¥400,000 per month, including prepaid severance pay and a fixed overtime allowance equivalent to 20 hours. MetLife said it views employees as the foundation of its business and plans to continue enhancing compensation and workplace benefits to create an environment where employees can work with stability and confidence.

item.title: Pinion Insurance selects Phinsys for finance infrastructure
item.description: Pinion Insurance has selected Phinsys to support the finance infrastructure behind its specialty insurance platform as the business scales its MGA operations. Phinsys will implement its finance automation platform to support Pinion’s finance operations, data integration, reconciliation, and reporting. “As we build a digital specialty insurance platform, having robust and scalable finance infrastructure is essential.

item.title: Tokio Marine receives SX Brand 2026 designation
item.description: Tokio Marine Holdings has been selected as an “SX Brand 2026” by Japan’s Ministry of Economy, Trade and Industry and the Tokyo Stock Exchange, becoming the only insurer included in this year’s group of 15 companies. This marks the first time Tokio Marine Holdings has received the SX Brand designation. Tokio Marine said it was recognized for linking the resolution of social issues with corporate value creation, while also achieving profit growth and volatility control through global risk diversification and unified group management.

item.title: AI boom’s supply chain trap
item.description: To view the full article and get unlimited access to InsuranceAsia News's exclusive insight across 5,000+ articles

item.title: Shadow Accounting in Insurance Compensation
item.description: Why shadow accounting happens in insurance Insurance compensation is uniquely difficult to manage because it sits at the intersection of money, relationships, compliance, and strategy. But in sales and compensation environments, shadow accounting usually looks less formal, consisting of spreadsheets, offline trackers, manually maintained commission logs, and side calculations used to validate or challenge official payout information. In insurance distribution, shadow accounting often appears when producers, managers, or back-office teams cannot easily see how commissions were calculated, whether adjustments were applied, or how hierarchy and eligibility changes affected payment.

item.title: Buenas Fuentes: Regulador mexicano multa alianza Banorte‑Rappi
item.description: Regulador mexicano multa alianza Banorte‑Rappi El regulador antimonopolio sancionó a Banorte y Rappi por concretar una alianza sin autorización previa . En diciembre de 2025 Banorte se hizo de todas las acciones de Tarjetas del Futuro. La sanción apunta al joint venture celebrado por las empresas para constituir en 2020 Tarjetas del Futuro, empresa que lanzó Rappicard.

item.title: Liberty Mutual Offers Auto Quotes in ChatGPT
item.description: The app lets ChatGPT users obtain auto insurance quotes from Liberty Mutual through a conversational experience designed to be more interactive than a traditional online form. (Image source: Liberty Mutual Insurance.) Liberty Mutual Insurance (Boston) has launched what it describes as a first-of-its-kind carrier-backed conversational AI auto insurance quoting application within ChatGPT. Quotes are available through the app for drivers in seven states, according to Liberty Mutual.

item.title: Verisk Report Shows Rising Roof Loss Severity
item.description: Verisk says everyday wind and hail events, many of which fall below catastrophe thresholds, continued to drive roof claim severity. Verisk says the report draws on property, claims and weather analytics to show how hail volatility and aging roof stock are increasing risk across the U.S. Roofs visibly in moderate to poor condition show approximately 60 percent higher loss costs than roofs in good or excellent condition, according to Verisk Roof Condition Score 2025 baseline data.

item.title: Guidewire’s Mullen Sees Core Systems Anchoring AI
item.description: For John Mullen, President, Guidewire (San Mateo, Calif.), the question is not simply how artificial intelligence will change insurance workflows. That view reflects Mullen’s role at Guidewire, where he oversees field functions including sales, customer success, professional services, solution and industry advisory, and field engineering. You have to be both if you want to navigate and lead in this industry.” Mullen sees that change emerging as many insurers move faster than they have during previous technology cycles.

item.title: Made-in-China EVs Enter Canada Under Deal Carney Struck With Xi
item.description: Chinese-made electric vehicles are beginning to arrive in Canada under a new arrangement that Prime Minister Mark Carney agreed to in January during a visit with China’s President Xi Jinping. The cars will be the first to be imported by Canada under a deal that allows as many as 49,000 Chinese EVs in a 12-month period at a tariff rate of around 6%. The Canada-China deal will be closely watched by U.S.

item.title: Stellantis Recalling 419K US Vehicles for Improper Side Air Bag Deployment
item.description: Chrysler-parent Stellantis is recalling 419,035 vehicles in the U.S. as a software error could delay the side air bags from deploying during a crash, the U.S. This would leave the vehicles non-compliant with U.S.

item.title: Warmer Global Temps to Create Bigger, More Damaging Hailstones: Study
item.description: A warmer world will likely make bigger and more damaging hail, a new study said. Because climate change from the burning of fossil fuels should make more high-energy unstable air, which is conducive to hail forming, global storms pelting roofs, cars and the ground with hail bigger than a large marble will increase between 38% and 47% by the end of the century, depending on how much heat-trapping gas the world spews, a study in Wednesday’s journal Nature said. “And that leads to more thunderstorms with updrafts capable of producing hail.” But with warmer air, there’s less cold as high up for smaller hailstones and they tend to melt more, where bigger ones don’t, Allen said.

item.title: Roof Costs Soar Even as Claims Decline: Verisk
item.description: Roof losses didn’t slow despite a 20 percent decline in overall claims volume in 2025. Roofs that are visibly in moderate to poor condition show approximately 60 percent higher loss costs than roofs in good or excellent condition, according to Verisk’s Roof Condition Score® (RCS) 2025 baseline data. Average residential roof replacement costs reached $17,631 in 2025, with repair costs averaging $4,699.

item.title: Court approves distribution of money recovered from Argento Wealth
item.description: In its 19 May 2026 judgment, the High Court approved pro rata distribution to eligible investors of money recovered by the FCA from Argento Wealth Limited (AWL). You can receive a share of the money recovered if you: We will make payments to eligible investors, as defined in the court judgment, who provide their bank account details on or before 1 August 2026 . If we do not have your bank details by this date, then you will be unable to receive a distribution payment.

item.title: The Week’s 10 Biggest Funding Rounds: Anthropic Dominates In An Otherwise Slower Week For Megarounds
item.description: This is a weekly feature that runs down the week’s top 10 announced funding rounds in the U.S. Check out last week’s biggest funding deal roundup here . The 5-year-old generative AI giant secured $65 billion in Series H funding this week, pushing its post-money valuation to a mind-blowing $965 billion.

item.title: Aspen Capital Markets fee income rose 11% in Q1 2026 to $50.6m
item.description: Aspen Capital Markets, the third-party, ILS and alternative reinsurance capital management unit that now falls under Sompo after its acquisition of the re/insurer, reported another increase in fee income generated from its work with third-party capital partners in the first-quarter of 2026. For Q1 2026, Aspen Capital Markets fee income is reported to have risen to $50.6 million, an 11% increase from the prior year’s $45.6 million. Recall that, Aspen Capital Markets grew its assets under management AUM by 23% to a new high of almost $2.73 billion over full-year 2025 .

item.title: Palomar extends earthquake reinsurance tower to $3.92bn at June renewal, cites ILS capacity
item.description: Palomar Insurance Holdings, the speciality US insurer that offers largely catastrophe exposed property products, has lifted the top of its reinsurance tower to provide as much as $3.92 billion of earthquake reinsurance protection at the renewals, with multi-year catastrophe bonds set to provide $1.28 billion of coverage for 2026. For its reinsurance renewal for the 2026-2027 year, Palomar has added $421 million in incremental limit, to lift its tower to the new $3.92 billion level, up from $3.53 billion a year ago. Much like in previous years, protecting against major earthquake events in its home state of California, as well as other quake-exposed regions of the US, remains a core focus of Palomar’s reinsurance program.

item.title: Aspen’s underwriting income hits $79.1m in Q1’26 as CoR improves
item.description: Aspen Insurance Holdings has reported a sharp improvement in underwriting performance for Q1 2026, with underwriting income almost tripling year-on-year to $79.1 million as the combined ratio improved to 89.1% from 96.1% a year earlier. Despite the stronger underwriting and operating performance, Aspen reported a net loss after tax of $55.6 million for the opening quarter of 2026, compared with net income of $36.8 million in Q1 2025. Aspen’s operating income in Q1 2026 rose to $85 million from $50.4 million in the same period of 2025, while net investment income edged higher to $77.5 million from $75.9 million.

item.title: Florida property cat reinsurance market ‘as strong as ever’, says Aon’s Chris Dittman
item.description: Chris Dittman, Executive Managing Director and Florida Practice Leader at broking group Aon, views the Florida property catastrophe reinsurance market as the healthiest and most stable it has ever been, pointing to stronger balance sheets, solid underwriting performance, increased capital levels, and continued stabilisation following recent reforms. During AM Best’s Analytical Briefing on the state of the Florida property market, Dittman stated that the current environment in Florida is very healthy, both from an insurance and reinsurance perspective. “The balance sheets are solidified, driven by meaningful underwriting margins, reduced loss costs, and strong rate adequacy.” Dittman continued, “Aon tracks 50 plus Florida focused personalised property carriers, and within that group, we’ve seen the statutory surplus levels grow from an aggregate of roughly $4.5 billion five years ago to over $9.2 billion this past year end.

item.title: Corpus Christi Focuses on Delaying Looming Water Crisis
item.description: Corpus Christi is bracing for demand to exceed water supplies by next summer, leaving far too little time to dodge a crisis by building new infrastructure, said Kenneth Dees, a water resources engineer based in Fort Worth. Five straight years of record heat, sporadic rainfall and divided leadership has Corpus Christi in danger of becoming the first U.S. At this point, he said, “the only thing that you could do is stop using water.” City leaders are left searching for ways to delay, not avoid, the looming emergency through conservation efforts, such as mandatory water restrictions and higher fees for exceeding limits.

item.title: Kentucky Food-Color Plant Was ‘Catastrophe Waiting to Happen,’ CSB Says
item.description: A Kentucky food-coloring manufacturer made a number of missteps and was not equipped to prevent the 2024 runaway chemical reaction that killed two workers and caused millions of dollars in damage to the plant and surrounding neighborhoods. “This tragic incident was a catastrophe waiting to happen,” Chemical Safety Board (CSB) Chairman Steve Owens said in a statement. “The reactor’s pressure relief system was not designed to release pressure from a reaction like this, and Givaudan did not recognize the potential for a runaway reaction to happen.” The CSB, an independent, nonregulatory federal agency that investigates incidents, explained that a reactor, used to produce caramel coloring for food products, had been relocated to the site from an older facility.
